description: I would recommend CATO to anyone looking for a networking solution out there. It's quite affordable and actually works. It makes the whole experience of using a VPN memorable.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: 1. Security - CATO VPN guarantees a secure connection when accessing client platforms 2. Single Sign On Feature saves a lot of login time (available for Windows, Google, etc) 3. CATO VPN is really fast and reliable compared to its competitors out there 4. They have an awesome support team. Always ready for feature requests.
cons: 1. The GUI can feel a bit cluttered. It's hard for a beginner to find what they are looking for 2. The Bulk/Mass user update feature is still missing 3. The CATO Client can keep on disconnecting sometimes

author: Elvison G.
pros: At the company I work for, we've used Cato Networks Suite to migrate from MPLS to SD-WAN, optimize global connectivity, improve secure access to SaaS applications, and enable secure hybrid work. This has allowed us to improve efficiency, reduce costs, and increase security in the network infrastructure. This is what I like about this tool.
cons: During the implementation of Cato Networks Suite, you may experience conflicts with third-party VPNs, antivirus software blocks, firewall issues, and installation errors. However, these issues can be resolved by adjusting the configuration and following the best practices recommended by Cato Networks.

review_title: beware of cancelation policy they do not allow this to be done even if its their fault
description: i was not happy with the service. their NOC always has a downtime (for philippines, not sure with other countries) at least once a month there is some sort of network issue when connecting with their NOC aside from the weekly downtime mentioned for maintenance issues. this is a SD wan product, so if thier side is down then you will be down as well even if you have multiple internet connections. defeats the purpose of redundancy. though you can switch from one of their NOCs to another NOC, the switching itself is a downtime and has to be manually done. when i decided to cancel my 5 year contract because of said issues, they are insisting i pay in full for the 3 years of service despite the fact that they violated SLA due to numerous downtimes etc. not happy at all and im still stuck now trying to figure out what to do with this pending bill they insist i should pay despite me cancelling.
author: michael B.
pros: easy deployment and simple admin panel to learn.
cons: you will have a weekly maintenance downtime of about 15 mins. they do update the software which is a good thing, but you will be down for that said period weekly which is a pain for operations who require 24/7.

review_title: A complete and proven SASE solution.
author: Muhammad B.
pros: The integration with our network straight forward.Dashboard provides a clear picture of top-level events.
cons: Support lacks the enthusiasm and can be improved.

review_title: Great for admins, Learning Curve for end users
description: Overall the experience with Cato has been great. Our prior VPN and firewall solution were hosted on-perm and back-hauling some traffic while split tunneling other was a consent game of cat & mouse. It has also solved a bunch of communication issues we had with our Site2Stie VPN. Converting them to Cato Nodes really improved performance.
author: Casey M.
pros: The solution was easy to install, configure, and deploy, from the administrators perspective. The ability to have it auto start when a user logs in to their endpoint has saved countless hours on my service desk troubleshooting the issues that arise when an endpoint is off the domain for too long.
cons: Currently the feature that gives me the most grief is the re-authentication sequence for the VPN client. If a user is actively using their system and the client goes to re-authenticate it opens a the SSO portal in a background tab in their browser, and then users are not sure how to proceed and our service desk gets calls. It would be nice if either the SSO portal was opened in the foreground, or there was "captive portal" behavior where if they tried to open up another site, they would get redirected to the SSO portal. Really it comes down to user training, but its not exactly intuitive as it stands.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Implementing Cato Networks has increased our security baseline footing by moving traffic management farther away from our internal network, decreasing the attack surface. It also strengthened our remote user access restrictions and control, and increased our internet bandwidth and network availability.
author: Andy K.
pros: - Edge firewall protection as a service rather than on-prem hardware that requires constant updating and lifecycle replacement/support. - Remote access methods that connects remote users directly to private backbone network and from there to resources. Lessens requirement for all traffic to flow through main office network for resource access, removes insecure split tunneling for internet access. - Ability to aggregate multiple WAN sources and software defined network QoS and traffic management, and redundancy should one WAN provider experience outage. - Local PoPs mean users connect to closest/fastest ingress to Cato Network, including Ashburn AWS PoP and MS data centers for our cloud-based resources. - AD directory sync to import VPN users and groups and also provide directory awareness (traffic can be labeled by specific AD user and/or machine).
cons: Like any other change between vendor offerings, there is a learning curve on migrating existing policies and routes/uses to the new setup (for example, firewall setups and NAT/port forwarding were different than our Fortinet equipment). Frequent usage and help from deployment specialists have lessened this and increased our self-reliance and understanding for day-to-day use.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: very good! What I like most is how easy and helpful it is to get customer support!
author: Josia C.
pros: The interface is very easy to use. During down time I get the notifications I need to make sure things are running, and overall it's a very reliable system
cons: It would be nice to make it easier to do network packet test on specific ip addresses to see if anything is getting blocked

description: A very easy to use and stable link, with plenty of services supplied; we need a carrier in a difficult region and CATO was the best solution in terms of bandwith, services, compliancy and time to deliver.
author: Ilario M.
pros: easy to use and mantain access everywhere very stable
cons: overall price, socket HW not always performing as expected

review_title: Cato Network Suit, a perfect solution to keep you safe anywhere
description: The experience has been good overall, we are able to work from anywhere like in the office, keep an eye of who is using the VPN solution, keep unwanted pages at bay to keep the team productivity.
author: Braian V.
pros: Easy enrollment of users, powerfull VPN, I can work anywhere like I'm in the office, the company can decide wich pages the users can use to avoid phisihng attacks or hacks via ads or unsafe pages
cons: When the platform has problems it can be a headache, this don't happen a lot, but it leaves users without a network unless they change the wireless network to a wired one.

review_title: Very Easy to Use and Install Software
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Cato integrates very smoothly with Windows 10 and is non-intrusive. Users will barely know it is running, except that everything works. From an Admin perspective, it has easy to use dashboards to get an overview of your services. The support from Cato through all stages of set up and use are frankly excellent.
cons: Nothing noticeably awry with this product.

author: Stephen T.
pros: Secure Access Service edge and ease of monitoring remote branches
cons: no Cloud Access Security broker feature

review_title: Great product with great support team
author: Urvish A.
pros: Light on system, easy to navigate and fast
cons: User friendliness of extracting various reports. This has dependencies from support

author: Enrique L.
pros: Easy to use, if your are looking for a simple environment, communication between branches and secure internet access this is the solution for you. If all of your sites are on the US.
cons: Lack of some features. It's really expensive for sites outside the US

review_title: Cato SDN is easy to setup and managed.
author: HAN NENG W.
pros: We like CATA SDN is the most user friend and ease of managing the SDN network. The network in the Asia Pacific regions were stable and able to help us to manage remote office location easily.
cons: Lack of support on the local remote location where our small region offices were located.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: We use Cato for routing and QoS over several sites and cloud datacenters, and the overall experience is great. We don't use instead security services, so I can't express myself for that. The product is inexpensive compared to MPLS or competitors, so it defintely worth the investment. Highly recommended.
author: Emiliano T.
pros: Cato offers a complete SASE suite that you can bring up really in a few hours. By choosing to connect your sites with the hardware appliance (Cato socket) you can have them online by simply connect the WAN port to the Internet. Alternatively, you can connect your exisiting infrastructure using IPSec or, in case of cloud subscriptions, using the virtual version of the socket. All the traffic and security rules can be defined using the cloud console, same thing for the network ranges to connect through the cloud router. Eveything is super-simple and super fast, leaving all the complexity of a global SD-WAN behind the curtain. Support team is available for any need and very fast in answers.
cons: QoS rules are applied at the Cato PoP, sometimes we had slight issues over the last-mile where the traffic shaper failed to avoid line congestion. Especially the "Limit only when the line is congested" feature resulted ineffective in those cases.

description: Some of the benefits I noticed migrating from Cisco firewalls / IPS / VPN and Velo SD-WAN to CATO SASE: - My connection to Azure is no longer a somewhat buggy Cisco ASA to Azure, site-to-site VPN, and the remote access VPN users no longer have to tunnel through the main datacenter to get to Azure. Instead with CATO, you can stand up a set of CATO virtual appliances in Azure. The virtual appliances connect out to the CATO cloud, your datacenter, offices and VPN users connect out to the CATO cloud, and all the things talk (if your WAN firewall rules allow). If you need a way to connect into Azure, this is a nice setup. - In the Cisco world, updating a firewall meant looking through several firmware versions, reading the release notes to make sure the firmware is compatible with the IPS and the VPN clients and the ASDM. The process always seemed overly complicated, especially when these systems are mission critical and outage windows are small. With CATO, firmware updates, IPS updates, SSL certs updates, VPN client updates are all done automatically. - The tech support so far has been good to work with. I always seem to have suggestions with any new security product and CATO has been receptive to them.
author: Jon D.
pros: I came to CATO from the Cisco world. I had 20+ years managing Cisco Firewalls/IPS/VPN/etc and one thing I required in a new solution was customization. The ability to fine tune security products to fit the specifics of different environments is critical in my opinion. CATO does require time to learn the system to get the most out of it, but it's no more difficult than Cisco firewalls.
cons: I'm very happy with CATO. One thing I would like to see in the future is the ability to write custom IPS rules.

review_title: Cato Networks Simply Removed All Concerns we used to have about Network connectivity and reliability
description: This has been one of the very best technology decisions we have ever made. After living with Cato Networks for a few years I can’t imagine switching to another provider
author: Gerry W.
pros: Deployment was quick and easy. After that we basically ignore it because it just works, all of the time. Problems and outages don’t happen any more. When we want to make a change we can do it quickly and easily and expert support is available 24x7 when we are doing something new and need some help
cons: There is very little to complain about and nothing major at all.

author: leonard C.
pros: Dashboard, the fluidity of navigating to where you want, the management piece of it, the help section but its mostly self explained to get to where you want to get to as long as you have some basic understanding. Some onboard training helps out as we had and once you use it you are pretty much up and running or use the help section
cons: Nothing really, it can be intimidating when you 1st see it but its really not. I suggest taking the time to really click through all the options and visualize how the options can protect your network

review_title: Cato's SASE offering is a top notch SDWAN/Security/Remote Access Solution-
description: Looking to combine SDWAN with a high quality network security solution, that has easy to use secure remote access software. CATO is able to accomplish this!
author: Chris B.
pros: I like it all. To be able to consolidate critical services into to ONE, with a single pane of glass to monitor and manage from...it's a winner. ZTNA is now needed more than ever, and to be able to include that with SDWAN, is a great way to go.
cons: I think the user interface could be made to look a little nicer and user friendly. Also, on smaller accounts, more user friendly billing terms would be helpful. Other than that, no issues about the service offering, the quality of the install and support teams, or the pricing. It's all top notch.

description: Overall experience has been great. Cato helped us get our workforce working from home when it was crucially needed.
author: Matthew C.
pros: Ease of setup, configuration, maintenance, and VPN.
cons: Every site requires a new bandwidth license which increases cost.

description: The best experience so far from a security supplier
author: Jason B.
pros: Has desired features, ease of use and integration with other apps.
cons: Want to be able to order direct and configure with CATO

review_title: Cato Networks Suite for small and medium Business
description: I have done a complete Network and infrastructure overhaul, Cato is a key component and it works well!
author: Thomas W.
pros: Ease of use, remote accessibility. Clear crisp and easy to use Dashboard.
cons: Reporting functionality - to cumbersome, not enough details no download capabilities.

author: Abhay S.
pros: Transparency, robustness, responsiveness
cons: Time lag between threat detection and response.

description: Very simple and agile solution. We really enjoy working with Cato.
author: Fernando L.
pros: Simplicity of how we can now control our communications and security.
cons: Cato pricing can be challenging and requires to evaluate internal communications and security costs to calculate a ROI.

review_title: easy to use, does what it says
description: great for vpn access, managing security on the network
author: Vickesh P.
pros: ease of use, easy to deploy, and administer intuitive interface
cons: cost was a negative, but you pay for what you get and the higher price does reflect the quality of the suite
